And as I've been making videos for you, I'm realizing that I keep asking you to do something that's pretty much physically impossible, which is to make ad decisions completely devoid of emotion, right? To not let yourself get freaked out and to not let yourself get overly excited. So if you spend 100 bucks and things don't work out, it's real easy for our brains to fast forward for the rest of our lives and be like, oh my god, I'm going to be bankrupt in the next five years if this keeps happening or whatever. And then on the other side, we spend 100 bucks or 500 bucks so we make $1,000. It's real easy for our brains to be like, woohoo. And then we just crank up the budget on the stuff that's working and it breaks it and then we freak out. And that's normal. And it takes a tremendous amount of self-discipline not to do that. And self-discipline is great, but it also kind of sucks because it's so hard to have. I don't have that much personally. Like they're usually overreport or underreport. High rows fixes that. So you're like, Oh, okay, this ad actually made this much money. Here's what I should do. So that's where it's truly beneficial. I have no idea why they called it that they're like, let's come up with the hardest to remember the spelling of domain ever period. Despite that, it's a freaking awesome tool. Love it. I use it been using it for years. What this thing does is it's got ad buying automation. So like I'll show you like automated stuff here in a minute. But it also uses artificial intelligence to do two things. Number one, find you little pockets of people to retarget. So remember, like we will build if you haven't watched this part already, you'll see me build a retargeting audience based on like a gazillion different things people can do with your ads and stuff. Well, it has AI that will build those audiences for you and then even segment them further for you, which is really, really cool. So you've got all these like a gazillion little micro audiences and stuff, which is really neat. So that's one cool thing it does. Another cool thing it does is it uses artificial intelligence to create look alike audiences of people who have done all kinds of different things in your business. So repeat buyers, repeat visitors, look alike. So people who have spent X amount of time on your site, look alike. So people who have watched X amount of sales videos by you, like all of these things you just press a button, it just makes the dang audiences for you, and then it'll launch a campaign to those audiences, which is like awesome, right? So that's great. So that's the pros of the thing. And it starts at 49 bucks a month. So like super cheap, you know, so it's worth using even if you don't use the automation just to freaking get that. But those audiences is really, really cool. And it integrates with Hyro. So it's another huge advantage to this thing. So if Hyros is getting super accurate data, and Magix can do automations based on that data that it's getting from Hyros, that means Magix can really do super accurate automation such as pause this ad if it's making me less than a 20% return on investment today, or whatever you want it to do, right? So that's like really, really, really cool. The downside is sometimes it takes a minute to load, you know, so it's just a big deal. But like sometimes a little like, you have to refresh it or something kind of frustrating. And you got to make sure the data that's coming into Hyros is accurate at first. And it might be right when you set it up. And if it's not, it's because they have to adjust some time zone stuff, they had to do that with me, the data was a little bit off, you know, so like Hyros would report one thing, and then Magix would report another, I was like, dude, what's wrong with this?
